events.title: Málaga CF vs. CD Castellón
events.description: This event is for the upcoming La Liga 2 game, scheduled for Saturday, April 25, 2026 between Málaga CF and CD Castellón.

eventMetadata.context_description: Málaga CF holds a slim trader consensus edge at home in LaLiga 2 against third-placed CD Castellón, driven by their top-tier home form averaging over two points per game and eight goals across the last five matches, yet the tightly bunched probabilities reflect Castellón's unbeaten run in six outings with 11 goals scored recently and a dominant head-to-head record, including a 2-1 win earlier this season. Both sides face key absences—Málaga without suspended Rafa Rodríguez and injured defenders Álex Pastor, Moussa Diarra plus midfielder Luismi, while Castellón misses suspended Alberto Jiménez and Beñat Gerenabarrena alongside injured Douglas Aurélio—heightening the defensive vulnerabilities in this crucial late-season promotion push.

question: Dougaz vs. Jubb: Match O/U 23.5
description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Aziz Dougaz and Paul Jubb in the Abidjan, originally scheduled for April 21, 2026 at 6:00AM ET.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed across all sets equals or exceeds 24. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
All tiebreaks—including any Champions/Super tiebreak—count as one (1) game toward the total.
If the match begins but is not completed, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins or delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a result, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official Challenger statistics.
groupItemTitle: Abidjan: Aziz Dougaz vs Paul Jubb Match O/U 23.5

events.title: Abidjan: Aziz Dougaz vs Paul Jubb
events.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Aziz Dougaz and Paul Jubb in the Abidjan, originally scheduled for April 21, 2026 at 6:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Aziz Dougaz' if Aziz Dougaz advances against Paul Jubb.

This market will resolve to 'Paul Jubb' if Paul Jubb advances against Aziz Dougaz.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Paul Jubb, seeded No. 4 and ranked No. 280, holds a clear edge over No. 538 Aziz Dougaz in their first-round clash at the Abidjan Challenger 50 on outdoor hard courts, reflecting trader consensus on Jubb's superior recent form with semifinal appearances in Hersonissos 2 and M15 Villena challengers/ITFs. Dougaz has notched challenger upsets like over Thiago Tirante but enters with mixed results including losses to Robin Bertrand and Sho Shimabukuro. Yesterday's rain delay interrupted play, pushing resumption to today amid forecasts of scattered thunderstorms that could impact conditions. No injuries reported; key watchpoints include Jubb's hard-court consistency versus Dougaz's left-handed serving threat in this early draw matchup.

question: Will Udinese qualify for the League Phase of the 2026-27 UEFA Europa League?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team clinches a league phase spot in the 2026-27 Europa League per UEFA rules. Otherwise, the associated market will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed team to clinch a league phase spot in the 2026-27 Europa League (e.g. they cannot mathematically achieve a Europa League place, cannot qualify through play in European or cup competitions, etc.), the associated market will resolve to "No". 

If the 2025-26 Serie A season is cancelled, postponed after August 31, 2026 11:59 PM ET, or the teams that qualified for the 2026-27 Europa League have not been officially confirmed by UEFA within that timeframe, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from UEFA and/or Serie A; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: Total Kills Over/Under 71.5 in Game 2?
description:
In the upcoming Round 3 match between Aurora and PARIVISION in the PGL Wallachia Group Stage, initially scheduled for April 20 at 11:40AM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 2.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 2 is 72 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.


In Dota 2, a "kill" for markets like team total kills, kill handicap, and interval kills is counted when an enemy hero dies and the game awards a hero kill event to the opposing team's score, which is the number shown on the in-game scoreboard and tracked in scoreboard feeds as team kills. In practical terms, this includes any enemy hero death that increments a team's kill score, regardless of who got the last hit, including situations where the victim dies to creeps, a tower, or neutrals as long as the kill is still credited to the enemy team. It does not include denies, such as killing your own allied units, and it also does not include building or objective destructions like towers, barracks, or Roshan, since those are tracked separately from kills. Assists also do not count as kills, because they are recorded as a separate player stat and multiple players can assist on a single kill.

events.title: Dota 2: Aurora vs PARIVISION (BO3) - PGL Wallachia Group Stage
events.description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 Round 3 match between Aurora and PARIVISION in the PGL Wallachia Group Stage, initially scheduled for April 20 at 12:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Aurora" if Aurora win the match against PARIVISION.

This market will resolve to "PARIVISION" if PARIVISION win the match against Aurora.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Aurora Gaming and PARIVISION, both 2-0 entering their PGL Wallachia Season 8 Swiss group stage BO3 clash on patch 7.41b, represent a marquee CIS matchup with playoff seeding on the line in the $1M Bucharest event. Aurora's gritty 2-1 comeback over Xtreme Gaming on April 19 showcased Nightfall's carry dominance and roster depth with recent additions Ws and kaori, fueling momentum. PARIVISION's flawless 2-0 shutouts against MOUZ and HEROIC emphasized SSS's offlane control under coach Puppey, backed by a 6-3 head-to-head edge including a 2-0 ESL One Birmingham playoff win last month. Draft flexibility and mid-lane duel between Mikoto and No[o]ne- loom as deciders in the aggression-favoring meta.

question: Trump out as President by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Donald Trump resigns or is removed as President or otherwise ceases to be the President of the United States for any period of time by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Donald Trump's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

Only permanent removal from office will qualify. Temporary removal (e.g. temporary invocation of the 25th Amendment under Section 3 or a Section 4 invocation not sustained by both Houses of Congress) or impeachment without removal will not count.

A sustained invocation of the Twenty-Fifth Amendment, Section 4 (i.e., if both Houses of Congress, by two-thirds vote, uphold the Vice President and Cabinet’s determination of presidential inability) will qualify for a "Yes" resolution. 

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Trump out as President by April 30?
events.description:

Only permanent removal from office will qualify. Temporary removal (e.g. temporary invocation of the 25th Amendment under Section 3 or a Section 4 invocation not sustained by both Houses of Congress) or impeachment without removal will not count.

A sustained invocation of the Twenty-Fifth Amendment, Section 4 (i.e., if both Houses of Congress, by two-thirds vote, uphold the Vice President and Cabinet’s determination of presidential inability) will qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices "No" at 99.4% for President Trump remaining in office through April 30, driven by Republican majorities in Congress that render impeachment conviction unattainable, requiring a two-thirds Senate vote amid unified GOP opposition. Democratic lawmakers filed House resolutions like H.Res. 353 last week, citing erratic Iran threats and public clashes, while urging 25th Amendment invocation over fitness concerns, but these face procedural irrelevance without bipartisan support. Recent White House activities, including executive orders signed April 18, underscore operational continuity amid Iran ceasefire talks. Realistic shifts would demand unprecedented events like VP Vance-led cabinet action, sudden resignation, or health crisis in the nine-day window.

question: Will the highest temperature in Miami be 92°F or higher on April 20?
description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Miami Intl Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 20 Apr '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Miami Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/fl/miami/KMIA.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

events.title: Highest temperature in Miami on April 20?
events.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Miami Intl Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 20 Apr '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Miami Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/fl/miami/KMIA.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Official observations from the Miami International Airport station (KMIA), the authoritative source for citywide records, confirm the highest temperature on April 20 reached 86-87°F, per Weather Underground's historical data aggregator drawing from National Weather Service automated sensors. This direct measurement—typically the peak afternoon reading under partly cloudy skies with northeast winds 10-20 mph—explains the market's overwhelming 100% implied probability on that bin, reflecting trader alignment with verified meteorology over preliminary reports suggesting lower peaks around 82°F earlier in the day. Persistent cool front remnants suppressed extremes beyond mid-80s, consistent with NOAA model consensus. Realistic challenges include minor post hoc quality-control revisions by NCEI, though such adjustments rarely exceed 1-2°F and would need to straddle bin boundaries to shift resolution. Final dataset expected within 24-48 hours.

question: Will the Audemars Piguet Index hit $44,000 (HIGH) by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, for any day between market creation and April 30, 2026, the Bloomberg x Subdial Watch Index for Audemars Piguet price is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source for this market is Subdial — specifically, the Audemars Piguet Index chart available at https://subdial.com/market. The daily values in USD shown on the chart will be used for resolution. 

This market refers to prices displayed in USD. To switch the display currency, use the currency toggle located at the left beneath the graph and select “USD” instead of “GBP”.

This market will resolve as soon as the price shown on the Bloomberg x Subdial Watch Index for Audemars Piguet chart reaches the listed price, or once the value for April 30, 2026, is published, and the listed price has not been reached. If no data for April 30, 2026, has been published by May 14, 2026, this market will resolve based on the data available at that time.

Revisions made to published data points on the Bloomberg x Subdial Watch Index for Audemars Piguet chart before the April 30, 2026 data point has been published will be considered; however, they will not disqualify a previously published data point from resolving this market. Revisions made after the April 30, 2026, data point has been finalized will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Bloomberg x Subdial Watch Index for Audemars Piguet, tracking secondary market prices of high-volume models, stands near $39,000 as of mid-April 2026, reflecting trader consensus on stabilization with a modest +0.6% gain over the past 30 days. This follows March's +0.6% advance amid a broader luxury watch recovery, where over 70% of major brands posted positive returns in Q1, driven by sustained collector demand and January's ~7% retail price hikes that reinforced secondary premiums. Value retention remains solid at +3.6% for popular Royal Oak models, though trading volumes signal caution in less liquid segments. With resolution imminent on April 30 data, end-month auction results or shifts in equity risk appetite could sway the index around key thresholds like $39,000–$41,000.

events.title: FC Fredericia vs. Odense BK
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Denmark Superliga game, scheduled for Sunday, April 26, 2026 between FC Fredericia and Odense BK.

eventMetadata.context_description: Odense BK holds trader consensus at 46.5% implied probability for victory over FC Fredericia in their Superliga relegation group matchup at Monjasa Park on April 26, fueled by second-place standing ahead of Fredericia's fourth, a gritty 1-0 win in the reverse fixture on April 6 via late Niemiec strike despite Fredericia's counter threats, and triumphs in the previous three head-to-heads. Fredericia's home form offers upset potential at 28.5%, but lacks clean sheets in 10 straight games and trails OB's sharper recent scoring (eight goals in last five matches versus six). Draw pricing at 26.5% reflects the closely contested relegation battle with both sides prioritizing points.
